brief_justification: Agent arrives at 4, not the correct answer of 2 — it correctly identifies non-window seats in a 3-3 layout but fails to account for how 8 passengers distribute across 12 seats (4 per row), leaving only 2 non-window seats in row 9; the analysis stays at surface level, identifying seat types without working through the group distribution math to find how many seats remain in row 9 specifically; the agent notices the contradiction (all 6 seats taken vs. 4 available for minor) but resolves it vaguely without re-examining its own logic error; no verification of the group split (4+4 across rows) or boundary check on how many non-window seats remain after group occupies their share of row 9; the agent presents 4 as the answer with confidence despite an unresolved internal contradiction about seat availability; only one seat-layout interpretation is used and no alternative distributions of the group of 8 (e.g., 5+3, 6+2) are considered; derivation shows seat labeling but the chain from group distribution to available-for-minor count is skipped, making reproduction of the answer from the text alone unreliable.
